name: codex-integrity
description: Stop-hook integrity gate, ON by default (adversarial mode): on every non-trivial turn it auto-runs an end-of-turn Codex (gpt-5.5) adversarial review and BLOCKS on anything flagged. Fail-open — if the codex CLI is absent/errors/times out it falls back to a do:change-skeptic advisory and never hard-blocks. Opt out with `/do:run codex off` (or `toggle`), which reverts to an advisory reminder only. Requires the codex CLI to perform the review. DISCLOSURE: this module sends the (secret-scrubbed) text of each reviewed turn to an EXTERNAL LLM (the local codex CLI / OpenAI). Turn text is routed through the shared secret-scrubber before egress (fail-closed: if scrubbing fails, nothing is sent), and codex runs READ-ONLY (--sandbox read-only) — it can read the repo to verify claims but cannot write, run network, or mutate machine state.

disclosure.dataSent: the assistant's turn text for the reviewed turn, secret-scrubbed before egress via lib/do-mon-context.js --scrub (fail-closed: if scrubbing is unavailable, no text is sent and the review degrades to the in-session do:change-skeptic)
disclosure.execution: read-only — codex runs with --sandbox read-only; it may read the repository to verify claims but cannot write files, access the network, or mutate machine state
